sourceMovie.title: Kabhi Khushi Kabhie Gham
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Kabhi Khushi Kabhie Gham (2001) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Karan Johar's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: What a Girl Wants
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kabhi Khushi Kabhie Gham clicked for you, What a Girl Wants (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Kabhi Khushi Kabhie Gham?
faq.answer: Try What a Girl Wants, Martian Child, Divine Secrets of the Ya-Ya Sisterhood—each shares DNA with Kabhi Khushi Kabhie Gham while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Kabhi Khushi Kabhie Gham?
faq.answer: Kabhi Khushi Kabhie Gham (2001) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Kabhi Khushi Kabhie Gham worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
